description: Ralph Loop pattern - Iterative self-improvement loop for AI agents. Uses /tmp/ralph_loop_test as test project.

tasks.name: Iterative Code Improvement
tasks.prompt: Implement a mini language interpreter step by step using TDD.

arguments.prompt:
You are in a Ralph Loop - an iterative self-improvement cycle.

Iteration: ${$.loop.iteration}
First iteration: ${$.loop.first}

You are building a mini language interpreter in Rust using TDD.
The code is in src/lib.rs with todo!() placeholders.

Your task:
1. Run: cargo test
2. Pick ONE failing test group to fix (Lexer → Parser → Interpreter order recommended)
3. Implement the missing functionality to pass those tests
4. Run tests again to verify
5. If more tests fail, continue in next iteration

Previous iteration result: ${$.loop.previous.improve.output}

IMPORTANT:
- When ALL 23 tests pass, output 'ALL_TESTS_PASSED'
- If tests still fail, output 'TESTS_FAILING' and summarize remaining failures

args.command: echo '${$.improve.output.content[0].text}' | grep -q 'ALL_TESTS_PASSED' && echo '{"completed": true}' || echo '{"completed": false}'
